_description: Per-question instrument-to-outcome trace. Each question mapped through all 5 layers: raw sources → statistical processing → rules applied → category → estimate → result

traces.text: Will Raúl Jiménez (Mexico) score a goal?

layer1_raw_sources.ESPN: Jimenez box scores from events 760415 (1G, 2SOT, starter vs RSA), 760441 (0G, 1SOT, starter vs KOR), 760467 (not a starter, rested vs CZE). Pulled from rosters[].roster[].stats.
layer1_raw_sources.Smarkets: ILLIQUID — no bid/offer present for any anytime_goalscorer market (event 45161448)

traces.layer2_processing: Thinned Poisson: lambda_MEX_goals=1.160 × 0.33 goal_share = lambda_jimenez=0.383 → P(scores)=1-exp(-0.383)=0.318
traces.layer3_rules: RULE5 (n=2 thin sample, trim), RULE12 (removing RSA collapses estimate, low confidence)
traces.verdict: Miss — submitted NO lean, outcome was YES. Thinned Poisson too conservative.

traces.text: Will the second half produce more goals than the first half?

layer1_raw_sources.Smarkets: Highest Scoring Half 3-way market (event 45161448): 2H=0.3978, Equal=0.3275, 1H=0.2769 — liquid

traces.layer2_processing: Direct market read — Second Half contract = 0.3978, rounded to 0.40
traces.outcome: NO (1H had more goals OR equal)
traces.verdict: Win — first half had more goals. Our estimate (0.40) vs crowd (0.49) reflects better calibration on half-goal distribution.

traces.text: Will Mexico have more shots on target than Ecuador?

layer1_raw_sources.Smarkets: MEX SOT OU3.5 Over=0.5407 → lambda_MEX=3.870. ECU SOT OU2.5 Over=0.5823 → lambda_ECU=3.025. Both liquid.

traces.verdict: Marginal loss — both we and crowd right on direction, crowd 2pp closer to YES outcome.

traces.text: Will the match have 2 or fewer total goals?

layer1_raw_sources.Smarkets: OU2.5 Under=0.6996 (liquid). OU3.5 Under=0.8624 (liquid). Mexico group record: 2+1+3=6G in 3 matches but ECU competitive avg only 1G per match.
layer1_raw_sources.ESPN: Mexico 0 goals conceded in all 3 group matches. ECU scored 2 goals in 3 matches (2G in just the GER match).

traces.layer2_processing: Direct market read: OU2.5 Under = 0.6996 → 0.70. Cross-check: lambda_total=1.966, Poisson P(<=2)=0.700. Perfect alignment.
traces.verdict: Win — strongest single YES call and it hit. Our 0.70 vs crowd's 0.61 shows we correctly identified the low-scoring structure of this match.

traces.text: Will Gonzalo Plata (Ecuador) score or assist a goal?

layer1_raw_sources.ESPN: Plata box scores: 0G/0A/1SOT vs CIV, 0G/0A/3SOT vs CUR (outlier), 1G/0A/1SOT vs GER. Card-prone: 4 fouls + 1YC vs GER. All from rosters[].roster[].stats.
layer1_raw_sources.Smarkets: ILLIQUID — no bid/offer for any player prop

traces.layer2_processing: Thinned Poisson: lambda_ECU=0.806 × 0.35 goal_share = lambda_plata=0.282 → P(scores)=0.246. P(score OR assist)≈0.321
traces.layer3_rules: RULE5 (trim for small sample), card-risk shade
traces.outcome: NO (Plata neither scored nor assisted — Mexico won to nil)
traces.verdict: Small win — identical estimates, slight numerical advantage when outcome was NO. Mexico clean sheet killed any ECU player props.

traces.text: Will Mexico have 6 or more shots on target?

layer1_raw_sources.ESPN: Mexico SOT history: 4 (vs RSA), 4 (vs KOR), 5 (vs CZE). Never reached 6 in any WC2026 group match.

traces.verdict: Strong win — 16pp gap on a NO outcome. ESPN history (4, 4, 5) was a concrete ceiling the crowd ignored. Key process lesson.

traces.text: Will a goal be scored from outside the penalty area?

layer1_raw_sources.Historical: WC knockout-stage base rate: ~8-12% of goals are from outside the box
layer1_raw_sources.ESPN: No direct ESPN metric for OTB goals. No Smarkets market available.

traces.layer2_processing: Base rate: expected_goals=2.0 × OTB_rate=0.10 = 0.20 expected OTB goals → P(>=1) = 1-exp(-0.20) = 0.18
traces.layer3_rules: None (no rules apply to base-rate questions)
traces.outcome: NO (no goal from outside the box)
traces.verdict: Win — crowd overpriced OTB goals at 27%. Our conservative base rate was rewarded.

traces.text: Will an own goal be scored?

layer1_raw_sources.Historical: WC2022: 6 OGs in 64 matches = 9.4% base rate. No Smarkets market.
layer1_raw_sources.ESPN: Mexico: 0 OGs in 3 group matches. No cross-heavy attack profile from ECU in competitive matches.

traces.layer2_processing: Base rate: WC OG rate ≈ 9.4%. Conservative trim given tight match and Mexico's clean record → 0.08
traces.verdict: Win — our lower base rate was correct. Crowd at 14% was overpriced.

traces.layer3_rules: None (FTR market already calibrated for draw probability)
traces.verdict: Loss despite correct direction — crowd at 0.48 was closer to the YES=1.0 outcome. We submitted direct market (0.43) but crowd was 5pp higher. If we had gone higher on Mexico, we would have beaten crowd.

traces.text: Will there be 9 or more total corner kicks?

layer1_raw_sources.ESPN: Mexico corners: 3, 1, 1 (avg 1.67 — lowest of any KO team). ECU competitive corners: 6 (vs CIV), 3 (vs GER) = avg 4.5.

traces.layer2_processing: Direct market read: OU8.5 Over = 0.4007 → 0.40. ESPN model would have given ~0.20-0.25 based on expected 6.2 total corners. Market overruled ESPN.
traces.layer3_rules: Direct market trust protocol (liquid market overrules ESPN-only model)
traces.verdict: Loss — both we and crowd underpriced corners. Crowd at 0.47 was less wrong. Mexico's corner count surprised. Knockout-round context (more set-pieces from physical contest) may inflate corner counts above group-stage history.

traces.text: Will there be 4 or more total cards?

layer1_raw_sources.ESPN: ECU YC: 1, 1, 3 (avg 1.67). MEX YC: 1, 0, 1 (avg 0.67). ECU fouls avg 11.67, MEX fouls avg 11.33.

traces.layer2_processing: Direct market: OU3.5 Over = 0.5371 → 0.54. ESPN confirms: combined 2.33 YC avg/game with physical profile of both teams.
traces.verdict: Win — our slight YES lean beat crowd. Ecuador's card-prone history (3 YC vs Germany) and both teams' physical fouling profile drove the YES.

traces.text: Will there be 3 or more offside calls?

layer1_raw_sources.ESPN: MEX offsides: 1, 3, 0 (avg 1.33). ECU offsides: 0, 1, 1 (avg 0.67). Combined avg 2.0/game. ECU STRUCTURAL NOTE: 0 offsides in their biggest competitive match (vs CIV); rarely caught offside even when pressing heavily.

traces.layer2_processing: Poisson: combined_lambda=2.0, P(>=3) = 1-poisson.cdf(2, 2.0) = 0.323. Shaded to 0.28 for RULE8 context (tight match = fewer forward runs) and ECU's structural low-offside profile.
traces.verdict: LARGEST SINGLE WIN: +49.58 RBP. 27pp gap between us (0.28) and crowd (0.55). Crowd priced offsides generically; we had Ecuador's match-specific ESPN data showing consistently low offside counts. This is the defining example of match-specific data beating generic crowd priors.

traces.text: Will a goal be scored before the first hydration break?

layer1_raw_sources.Smarkets: Goal 1 time bracket (briefly liquid then suspended): 1-10=14.9%, 11-20=14.5%, 21-30=12.2%. Also: 1H OU0.5 Over=0.545.

traces.layer2_processing: Time-window Poisson: lambda_window=0.546 → P=0.421. Time bracket sum: 1-10+11-20+partial21-25 ≈ 0.354. Central estimate: 0.40.
traces.layer3_rules: TIMING_EARLY category (reliable per knockout deep-dive) — trusted near-raw
traces.outcome: YES (goal scored before first hydration break)
traces.verdict: Win — goal came early. Our 0.40 (vs crowd's 0.34) from time-window Poisson was closer to truth. TIMING_EARLY category maintains perfect record.

traces.text: Will a penalty kick be awarded?

layer1_raw_sources.Smarkets: Penalty Yes=0.258 (wide spread: bid=0.2083, offer=0.3077) — liquid but wide

traces.verdict: Marginal win — 1pp below crowd when outcome was NO. Small positive contribution.

traces.text: Will there be 20 or more total shots?

layer1_raw_sources.Smarkets: Shots OU20.5 Over=0.521 → lambda=20.91. Shots OU21.5 Over=0.465 → lambda=21.26. Both liquid.
layer1_raw_sources.ESPN: MEX group avg shots 11.67 + ECU competitive avg shots 9.5 = ~21.2 combined. Confirms market lambda.

traces.verdict: Win — dual-market lambda cross-validated by ESPN team averages gave us higher conviction than crowd. Both sources pointed to ~21 shots expected.
